status_detail: Functioning normally — triggered by Presidential Major Disaster Declaration
eligible_applicants: State, local, tribal in declared disaster areas
key_eligible_uses: Warning systems, evacuation improvements, infrastructure hardening — flexible post-disaster money
window_note: Projects generally due by March 31 following the fiscal year of the event
sales_note: THE post-disaster play: any county (or neighbor) with a declaration in the last 24 months is sitting on flexible mitigation funds. Lead outreach with their own declaration number.

status_detail: Terminated April 2025; court ordered restoration Dec 2025; FEMA non-compliant as of early 2026 — legal limbo, no active NOFO
window_note: No active NOFO — do NOT build a deal on BRIC
sales_note: Do not pitch BRIC. If a prospect's project was stranded by the cancellation, pivot them to HMGP (if declared) or NGWSGP/SHSP.

program_name: HHPD (Rehabilitation of High Hazard Potential Dams)
status_detail: Funded via National Dam Safety Program; allocations flow through state dam safety offices
key_eligible_uses: Early-warning systems, downstream voice sirens, evacuation mapping below dams
sales_note: Public Works / dam-safety play: funds the exact fixed acoustic arrays + evacuation zones needed below high-hazard dams. Find dams in the prospect's county first (NID database).

program_name: USDA Community Facilities Direct Loan & Grant
status_detail: Rolling applications year-round through USDA RD state offices
key_eligible_uses: Public safety equipment, warning sirens, emergency vehicles, communications hardware
sales_note: The rural play: communities under 20k that fail every FEMA competitive test still qualify here. Grants up to 75% in lowest-income communities, plus low-interest loans.

key_eligible_uses: Hiring, community policing technology and equipment
sales_note: Position LRAD as de-escalation technology (voice before force). Best as secondary funding for PD deals.
